title: Lacquered Mustard Irish Cabbage Bake
description: A comforting, low-carb Irish staple featuring tender cabbage wedges coated in a sticky, savory mustard glaze with melted butter. This dish provides a chewy, satisfying texture perfect for recovery or cold evenings.

ingredients.name: Green Cabbage
ingredients.notes: cut into 8 thick wedges with core intact

instructions: Preheat your oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit and grease a large rimmed baking sheet.
Whisk together the melted butter, both mustards, vinegar, sweetener, garlic powder, and pepper in a small bowl.
Arrange the cabbage wedges on the baking sheet ensuring they do not overlap.
Brush half of the mustard mixture generously over the tops and sides of each cabbage wedge.
Cover the tray tightly with aluminum foil and bake for 20 minutes to tenderize the leaves.
Remove the foil and brush the remaining mustard glaze over the cabbage wedges.
Return the tray to the oven uncovered and bake for another 15 minutes until the edges are charred.
Baste the cabbage with the rendered butter from the pan halfway through the final baking time.
Switch the oven to broil for the last 2 minutes to achieve a sticky lacquer finish.
Serve immediately while the butter is bubbling and the cabbage is soft yet chewy.
